feed.title: Geopolitics
feed.subtitle: Combined think-tank feed: ISW, RUSI, CSIS, and the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace.

entry.title: What We Know About the U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal
entry.content: Despite the agreement’s uncertainty, it’s still a significant development in regional and nuclear policy.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Burnham Has a Narrow Window to Shape UK AI Policy
entry.content: His challenge will come in balancing domestic priorities with a sharpening geopolitical environment.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: A Path Forward on AI Safety for the United States and China
entry.content: “AI safety in parallel” is modest and pragmatic but vital.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Negotiating an Age of Conquest: Converting American Power Into Durable Political Outcomes
entry.content: There is a deeply rooted assumption in post–Cold War American statecraft that overwhelming military and economic power translates directly into favorable political outcomes. But force alone is rarely enough. To meet the challenges of the mid-21st century, Washington needs a cultural shift toward a mindset that takes negotiation far more seriously.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: The Alternative für Deutschland and Germany’s Unsettled Place in the World
entry.content: Unresolved tensions within the AfD may ultimately constrain the its potential as a leader among European and global populist forces.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: A Competitive Analysis of the Biomanufacturing Strategic Landscape and Technology Stack
entry.content: Biomanufacturing—the ability to deploy biotechnology for manufacturing—is rapidly emerging as a global economic and national security priority.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Why Europe Must Take Charge of its Russia Diplomacy Now
entry.content: Trump is distracted and Ukraine wants Europe to step up. The continent’s leaders must find their voice and assert it in talks with Russia.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: India’s Stake in the Global Subsea Cable Race
entry.content: The Indian Ocean Region hosts one of the densest concentrations of subsea cables in the world. Yet, despite accounting for a significant share of global internet traffic, India’s participation in the ownership, routing, and maintenance of this network remains limited.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Detour Dividends: How Syrian Airspace Benefits from the Iran War
entry.content: This installment of Charting the Middle East spotlights the unlikely positive implications of flight disruptions for Syria in wake of the Iran war.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: How the U.S. Can Survive a Large-Scale AI Cyberattack
entry.content: Advances in offensive AI cyber capabilities are threatening U.S. cybersecurity. The U.S. must improve its whole-of-society cyber readiness through enhancing its partnerships and communication before a major AI cyber-attack occurs.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Will the U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Proceed?
entry.content: President Trump's last-minute link between the U.S.-Saudi nuclear agreement and Israel normalization injects new uncertainty into an already contentious deal.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 23, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 23, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 23, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 23, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Losing Control of the West’s Longstanding Interests in Seychelles
entry.content: A democratic and well-functioning international financial centre that has benefited from Western support, Seychelles seems to now be choosing its friends elsewhere.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: North Africa’s Flaring Problem is Europe’s Gas Opportunity
entry.content: The fallout from the Iran War has not been kind to European economies. A UK leadership change presents an opportunity to invest in combatting gas flaring in North Africa, so as to bolster security of supply, and tie ongoing international diplomatic efforts on Libya to that agenda.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Where Are President Tokayev’s Political Reforms Leading Kazakhstan?
entry.content: Tokayev’s predecessor, Nursultan Nazarbayev, outlined arrangements for a power transition too soon and in too much detail, ultimately losing control over the process. Tokayev is determined not to meet the same fate.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: States Weekly Newsletter: July 22, 2026
entry.content: This edition of States Weekly covers July 22, 2026.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Charting a New Course: Countering China's Dominance in Global Shipbuilding
entry.content: China has developed the largest shipbuilding industry in recent history and now uses it as an instrument of national power. It is key to Beijing's ambitions to displace the United States as the preeminent power in the Asia-Pacific and project power far from China’s shores.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Section 232 Explained
entry.content: In this week's column, Bill Reinsch explains how Section 232 national security investigations have become popular trade tool of the Trump administration, arguing that while some tariffs are difficult to justify, recent decisions suggest a more selective approach to their use.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Tracking Global Compulsory Licensing Trends in Biopharmaceuticals
entry.content: From HIV/AIDS to Covid-19, global health emergencies have revived calls for compulsory licensing. But history shows that innovation, technology transfer, and manufacturing capacity—not patent waivers alone—drive access to lifesaving medicines.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: What Do the Election Security Documents Say About Chinese Interference in U.S. Elections?
entry.content: The biggest takeaway from the declassified intelligence isn't a cover-up—it's how intelligence analysis actually works. Healthy disagreement, documented dissent, and rigorous debate are signs of a strong intelligence community, not a broken one.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: AI Strategies and the Informal Economy: Africa’s Job Creation Test
entry.content: African nations have implemented AI strategies that focus on their formal sectors, leaving the informal economy behind.
entry.dc:creator: CSIS

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 22, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 22, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 22, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 22, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russia’s Selective Adaptation: Command, Control and the Limits of Learning in War
entry.content: The contrast between Russia’s tactical flexibility and operational rigidity is revealing of the insecurity in its executive function.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Japan's Ambivalent Policy Towards Russia and Ukraine
entry.content: Prime Minister Takaichi’s equivocal stance on policy towards Russia has encouraged a revival in activity from those in Japan who seek reengagement with Moscow.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Is AI as Bad for the Environment as Everyone Thinks?
entry.content: That statistic about a bottle of water may not live up to scrutiny.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Building Mini-Refineries Will Not Solve Russia’s Gasoline Crisis
entry.content: Official discussions about mini oil refineries are unlikely to come to anything—but the mere fact they’re happening reveals the regime is failing to deliver a functioning economy.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Why Russia Could Escalate Hybrid Warfare Against Europe
entry.content: Mounting economic strain and battlefield losses will not necessarily make the Kremlin less dangerous. They could instead push Moscow toward a more aggressive hybrid campaign designed to test NATO’s Eastern flank, exploit allied hesitation, and fracture European resolve.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Korean Peninsula Update, July 21, 2026
entry.content: Korean Peninsula Update, July 21, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 21, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 21, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 21, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 21, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: The Dismissal of Ukraine's Defence Minister
entry.content: Fedorov's removal leaves critical issues unresolved between the Defence Ministry and General Staff.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: In the Trump Gaza Plan, Diplomatic Failure is Success
entry.content: By failing to address Palestinian representation, the current process is bound to lead to just another temporary arrangement.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 20, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 20, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 20, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 20, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Prince Philip Lecture 2026: On Science and National Security
entry.content: This year's lecture explores the significance of national security and defence skills, and the role of universities in educating the next generation of engineers and scientists.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: Andy Burnham’s International Debut: Key Challenges and Opportunities
entry.content: Prime Minister Andy Burnham faces a challenging international context, with intractable problems, but also opportunities to lead and exert UK influence on world events.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 19, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 19, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 19, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 19, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 18, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 18, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 18, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 18, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: China & Taiwan Update, July 17, 2026
entry.content: China & Taiwan Update, July 17, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 17, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 17, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 17, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 17, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: RUSI Reflects: Andy Burnham's Geopolitical Priorities as New UK Prime Minister
entry.content: With Andy Burnham set to become the next UK prime minister, RUSI's new director of international security evaluates the new leader's most pressing geopolitical priorities.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: Putin's Fuel Dilemma
entry.content: Ukraine's deep-strike campaign is putting pressure on Russia's energy infrastructure and forcing increasing political pain for Putin.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Carnegie’s Summer Beach Reads
entry.content: Carnegie experts recommend the books that kept them turning pages—and learning along the way.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Taking the Pulse: Enough with the Annual NATO Summits, Already?
entry.content: Over the past ten years, NATO has held almost as many summits as it did during the entirety of the Cold War. Are they still useful, or is it time to stop holding annual meetings?
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 16, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 16, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 16, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 16, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: The Algorithmic Deterrent
entry.content: Palantir’s manifesto and the sovereignty question for states without algorithmic independence.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: The UK's Defence Investment Plan Has a Lethal Drone Swarm Vision
entry.content: Drone swarming is often referenced as an important future capability, with little understanding of what is possible today, and how the tactic is enabled.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: What’s Driving the Trump Administration’s Push to Dismantle the ICC?
entry.content: The move is an escalation of its war on multilateralism.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Europe from Scratch: Visions for a New European Order
entry.content: Several leaders have called for major EU reforms, but only modest changes have resulted. What if the European order really could be reimagined from zero today?
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: What Should We Take From Andrey Melnichenko’s Essay in the Economist?
entry.content: Andrey Melnichenko’s essay offers no answer to the fundamental question of how, under any kind of negotiated settlement, Europe can protect itself from the Russian ressentiment that is inevitable in all scenarios except for an outright victory for Putin.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: AI in Outer Space: Opportunities, Risks, & Governance Gap
entry.content: How is AI reshaping space security, creating governance challenges, and where does international diplomacy stand today?
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 15, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 15, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 15, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 15, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Cheap Attack, Expensive Defence: NATO 5% Pledge Following Iran and Ukraine
entry.content: Iran and Ukraine show cheap mass defeats expensive platforms. Unless it buys deep, low-cost defence and civilian resilience, NATO can win every engagement and still lose wars.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: NATO Planning for War with Russia – but has Moscow Left the Door Open?
entry.content: Moscow’s strategic communications cast Russia as a backseat passenger on a road to war in Europe – but war is not inevitable.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: How NATO Became Anchored in the Black Sea
entry.content: NATO countries Turkey, Romania, and Bulgaria are upgrading their fleets and deepening trilateral cooperation in the Black Sea
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: European Sycophancy Worked on Trump
entry.content: Nearly a year and a half after Europeans leaned into sucking up to Trump, the strategy has produced some benefits when it comes to Ukraine.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Korean Peninsula Update, July 14, 2026
entry.content: Korean Peninsula Update, July 14, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 14, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 14, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 14, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 14, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Episode 22: Delivering National Security Strategies
entry.content: Former UN Ambassador and National Security Advisor Sir Mark Lyall Grant discusses the challenges facing delivery of Western security strategies.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: Recording: FATF Greylisting and Financial Inclusion
entry.content: This webinar explores the relationship between FATF 'greylisting' and financial inclusion, drawing on the Centre for Finance and Security's recent research and policy discussions.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: What’s Left of NATO’s Asia Engagement?
entry.content: Shared interests among European and Asian middle powers, navigating a turbulent Sino-American unilateralism, sustain NATO’s Asian partnerships.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Thinking Beyond the Baltics for Deterring Russia
entry.content: A rebalancing of the NATO Alliance should not involve Europe replacing US capabilities on a like-for-like basis.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 13, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 13, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 13, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 13, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Defence Spending Must Become Defence Capacity
entry.content: The Defence Investment Plan shows where more money may go. The harder test is whether ministers can show how spending, assets, maintenance and liabilities become readiness.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 12, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 12, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 12, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 12, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Iran Update Special Report, July 11, 2026
entry.content: Iran Update Special Report, July 11, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 11, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 11, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 10, 2026
entry.content: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 10, 2026
entry.dc:creator: ISW

entry.title: Episode 124: After Ankara: First Reflections on the 2026 NATO Summit
entry.content: The 2026 NATO Summit convened at a moment of fragile unity for the alliance. What did it deliver? What did it leave unresolved?
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: The AI-Enhanced Land Carrier Battle Group: The Way Forward
entry.content: The historical evolution of naval aviation offers useful ideas for overcoming the attritional nature of current drone and AI-dominated land warfare
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Publications

entry.title: 25 Years After 9/11. The Next Global Shock Could be Infinitely Worse
entry.content: The next global shock may be the use of nuclear weapons assisted by the more casual attitudes of less responsible world leaders and the lack of popular understanding and fear.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Egypt’s Military Landlord Economy and its Limitations
entry.content: The armed forces champion a form of capitalism that is generating revenue, but its reliance on rent faces diminishing returns, leaving the country with massive sunk costs and deferred returns, deepening dependency on external borrowing.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Securing America’s Near Abroad: Recalibrating U.S. Policy Toward Haiti
entry.content: Helping to stabilize Haiti is in the United States’ national interest and can be done by making wise use of various foreign policy tools in addition to supporting international security forces.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Demystifying the Nuclear Threshold
entry.content: The nuclear weapons threshold is increasingly important for proliferation strategy and policy. Policymakers should better understand the implications of the threshold phenomenon in the current international security environment and plausible strategies to deal with the growing challenge that it presents.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: From Hormuz to the Maghreb: The Geopolitical Reach of a Gulf Crisis
entry.content: Morocco and Algeria, each in its own way, are having to navigate the global economic fallout of the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: The Democratisation and Diversification of War
entry.content: Reflections on the effects of technological change and normative retrenchment on the character of contemporary war.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: The Costs of Moral Pretence: How Hypocrisy Undermines International Order
entry.content: When the United States proclaims moral superiority while acting inconsistently with those moral claims, it pays a measurable price in global cooperation and international legitimacy.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: East Asia’s Re-Ordering Milieu After 1945: Beyond the San Francisco Myth
entry.content: East Asian order should not be conflated with U.S. strategy in Asia.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Crime, Violence, and the Reshaping of Politics in the Americas
entry.content: Latin America is undergoing a political transformation driven as much by the perception of insecurity as by its reality.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: The Future of Russian Sanctions After the Hormuz Crisis
entry.content: US–Iran peace negotiations have stumbled, but if normalcy returns to the Strait of Hormuz, the G7 must be ready to align again on pressuring Russia.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Threats to Maritime Trade are Spreading to the Indo-Pacific
entry.content: The ‘Bashi Breakout’ – China’s maritime operation east of Taiwan – is a warning that disruption to maritime trade is spreading to the Indo-Pacific.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: China’s Grey-Zone and Geoeconomic Coercion Below the Threshold of Conflict
entry.content: China’s ‘war by other means’ is there to see, if Europe looks past isolated incidents.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Network and Structural Power: The Four Trend Lines Weakening U.S. Leverage
entry.content: Networks—from international payments platforms to key economic sectors—underlie many aspects of U.S. power. But they are suffering under an extractive approach to foreign policy.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Ukraine’s Logistics Targeting Raises Questions for Russia’s Rear Defences
entry.content: Ukraine’s use of drone warfare has serious implications for Russian lines of communication.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: When AI Agents Attack: Autonomous Cyber Operations and Europe’s Governance Gap
entry.content: Autonomous AI agents are increasingly prevalent in cyberspace. The EU needs a real-time monitoring strategy, to invest in AI defenses, and to reduce its strategic dependence on U.S. frontier models.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Will American Power in Europe Run Out?
entry.content: American power is entrenched in Europe. Yet the depth of this relationship has become a source of unease in Europe and Europeans are working to reduce their exposure to the vicissitudes of U.S. politics wherever they can.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: NATO 3.0 to Emerge at Ankara Summit Amid Fragile Unity
entry.content: European allies and Canada will try to ease recurring tensions with President Trump by stepping up in a rebalanced alliance: NATO 3.0. But transatlantic unity remains fragile.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Reforming European Security: A Turkish Perspective
entry.content: The Alliance is being pulled toward a NATO 3.0 model by two structural drivers: the changing character of warfare, made visible by the war in Ukraine, and the redistribution of transatlantic burden-sharing as the United States gives greater priority to homeland security and the Indo-Pacific.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Ramzan Kadyrov: The Kremlin’s Messenger to the Islamic World
entry.content: The reported failing health of Ramzan Kadyrov leaves a strategic communications gap for a Kremlin speaking to the Islamic world.
entry.dc:creator: RUSI Commentary

entry.title: Nuclear Weapons and the Future of American Power
entry.content: It seems likely that, no matter what, the power of the U.S. nuclear arsenal will face erosion, not least in the credibility of its commitments to defend allies and the political durability of those alliances.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment

entry.title: Threading the Needle: India’s Path Forward with China
entry.content: This paper argues that there are predominantly four imperatives guiding India’s approach to China, and they exist in an order of priority.
entry.dc:creator: Carnegie Endowment
